title: Language Learning Hours Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Long Does It Take to Learn a Language?
description: Estimate weeks and hours to reach B2 level. Free calculator using FSI guidelines for 8+ languages. Based on study hours per week.
intro: Learning a new language requires sustained exposure and consistent practice. According to the Foreign Service Institute (FSI), for English speakers: Romance languages (French, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese) need ~600-750 hours; German requires ~1,100 hours; Japanese, Mandarin, and Arabic need ~2,200 hours. Key principle: consistency beats intensity. This calculator estimates hours to reach B2 (Upper-Intermediate) level. Data updated to 2026 with periodic reviews.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **FSI/CEFR guidelines**. Direct calculation based on your input values. Estimates hours needed to reach B2 (Upper-Intermediate) level in your target language.

explanation:
**Learning a language** requires sustained effort spanning hundreds of hours. Cognitive science principles—including spaced repetition (Ebbinghaus), immersion, and active practice—form the foundation established by FSI and Cambridge language institutions.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator estimates the hours required to reach B2 (Upper-Intermediate) level based on your current proficiency, weekly study hours, and target language difficulty. The formula applies FSI guidelines directly, checking for typical ranges and alerting when inputs fall outside reasonable expectations.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference estimate only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically for accuracy.

faq.q: How long does it take to reach B2 level in a language?
faq.a: It depends on the language difficulty and hours per week. Romance languages need 600-750 hours, Germanic languages 1,100 hours, and tonal languages like Mandarin or Arabic need 2,200+ hours.
The Foreign Service Institute (FSI) classifies languages by difficulty for English speakers and estimates study hours needed for proficiency levels.

faq.q: Which languages are easiest for English speakers to learn?
faq.a: Category I (600-750 hours): French, Italian, Portuguese, Spanish, Dutch. These share Germanic or Romance roots with English.

faq.q: Which languages take the longest to learn?
faq.a: Category IV (2,200+ hours): Mandarin Chinese, Japanese, Arabic. These have different writing systems and complex grammar structures.

faq.q: What does the CEFR level B2 mean?
faq.a: B2 (Upper-Intermediate) means you can communicate fluently on a range of familiar topics, understand main points in complex texts, and express opinions with supporting reasons.

faq.q: Is 8 hours per week enough to learn a language?
faq.a: Yes. Consistency matters more than intensity. 8 hours per week (~1 hour daily) is far more effective than sporadic 20-hour weekend sessions.

faq.q: Does language immersion really accelerate learning?
faq.a: Yes. Studies show immersion (living in-country) can speed up learning 3-5x by providing daily context, native input, and practical necessity.

faq.q: How do I use this calculator?
faq.a: Select your target language, enter hours per week, choose your current level, and the calculator shows weeks and total hours needed to reach B2.
Yes, all Hacé Cuentas calculators are free and require no registration or sign-up.
